behavior_summary: This script implements a minimal "U8P_NSSTICKS::look" (examine) handler for an object/NPC. When the player examines the entity, the handler plays a short bark saying "sticks " and then suspends/ends the U8P_NSSTICKS::look process.
There are no menus, branching conditions, parameter usage, external calls, or global flag modifications in this script. The behavior is a single linear examine-response.
notable_control_flow: Entry into the process immediately emits a bark/dialogue line (text: "sticks ").
The process then suspends/terminates (no further logic or branching).
open_questions: Is the entity an item or an NPC in-game (metadata does not distinguish explicitly)?
Does the trailing space in the bark text "sticks " have gameplay significance or is it an artifact of extraction?
Are the raw Unknown nodes ("{" and "suspend") purely structural/decompiler remnants or do they reflect any additional subtle engine behavior (e.g., timing/yield) that matters to game logic?

main_function.name: U8P_NSSTICKS::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description when the player examines the object/NPC by emitting a single short bark and then suspending the U8P_NSSTICKS::look process.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player issues a 'U8P_NSSTICKS::look' / examine action on this object/NPC (process type 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_NSSTICKS::look
function_map.role: Examine/U8P_NSSTICKS::look handler that emits a single bark and suspends
